summary: Experimental async-observer and async-observable support for scenarios that need deeper async/await semantics than classic Rx.
recommendedPatterns: Use AsyncRx only when asynchronous observer semantics are required, not by default.
Keep the distinction between IAsyncObservable<T> and IObservable<T> explicit.
Evaluate whether classic Rx or IAsyncEnumerable<T> already solves the problem before introducing AsyncRx.
avoidPatterns: Do not adopt AsyncRx just because a pipeline contains async methods.
Do not blur experimental async-reactive abstractions with mainstream Rx without a clear reason.
setupSteps: Confirm that async observer semantics are truly needed.
Reference System.Reactive.Async only for targeted scenarios.
Document why classic Rx or IAsyncEnumerable<T> was insufficient.
commonPitfalls: Adding AsyncRx where normal async composition around Rx would be simpler.
Failing to explain why async observers are part of the design.
